one_line_takeaway: Luminark trades a small amount of robustness/fidelity versus diffusion-only certified watermarks (Gaussian-Shading, PRC-W) for the first probabilistically-certified, training-free watermarking scheme that plugs into any generative paradigm exposing a guidance mechanism (diffusion, autoregressive, and AR-diffusion hybrids alike).
key_finding: On ImageNet models, Luminark achieves >=95% average detection accuracy across all 9 transformation types on all 9 models (e.g., EDM2-XS average 96.21%, VAR-d16 average 95.81%, MAR-Base average 96.56%), competitive with but generally slightly below the diffusion-specific certified baselines Gaussian-Shading (~99.5% average across EDM2 variants) and PRC-W (~95.9-97.4% average on EDM2), and substantially above classical post-hoc baselines DwtDct (~58%) and DwtDctSvd (~65%). Critically, Gaussian-Shading and PRC-W are diffusion-only and reported 'Not Applicable' for VAR and MAR in the fidelity table, whereas Luminark works across all 9 architectures. On fidelity (FID, lower is better), Luminark closely approaches the unwatermarked reference on every model (e.g., EDM2-XXL: Luminark FID 2.13 vs. Ref 1.81 vs. Gaussian-Shading 4.87 vs. PRC-W 4.60; VAR-d30: Luminark 3.06 vs. Ref 2.05, with GS/PRC-W not applicable to VAR/MAR since they are diffusion-specific), and on Stable Diffusion 2.1 / MS-COCO, Luminark FID 26.00 vs. Ref 26.83 vs. Gaussian-Shading 26.27 vs. PRC-W 26.33, with CLIP score 0.3320 vs. Ref 0.3329. Ablations (Table 4, FID on EDM2-L/VAR-d30/MAR-Large) show post-hoc projection (FID 16.70/7.68/6.88) and hard step-wise projection (FID 92.05/33.25/85.15) both severely degrade quality relative to Luminark's guidance approach (2.72/3.06/3.32), and that the luminance channel outperforms R-only, G-only, B-only, RGB-average, and random-linear-combination watermark variants on FID for the same three models.
core_contribution: Luminark, a training-free watermarking method for vision generative models (diffusion, autoregressive, and hybrid). The paper's own contributions are two: (1) a new watermark definition based on patch-level luminance statistics -- an image is partitioned into non-overlapping k x k patches, each patch's luminance l(p_i)=0.299R+0.587G+0.114B is compared against a provider-chosen threshold tau_i to produce a binary sign pattern, and a match rate m(x,W) against a secret target pattern c is used for detection; and (2) 'Watermark Guidance' (WG), a training-free injection mechanism that repurposes the classifier/classifier-free guidance term already present in modern diffusion/AR/hybrid samplers, replacing or augmenting it with the gradient of a hinge-like penalty Penalty(x,W) so the sampling trajectory is nudged toward satisfying the target luminance pattern without retraining the generative model.
threat_model: Not an adversarial-attack paper; it is a provenance/detection mechanism paper. The 'threat' addressed is: (a) an unwatermarked/natural image being falsely flagged as watermarked (false positive), which the paper bounds statistically via Proposition 1 (a Hoeffding/Chernoff bound showing Pr[m(x,W) >= 1/2+eps] <= exp(-N*D_KL(eps)) when c is i.i.d. Bernoulli(1/2) and tau is arbitrary, kept secret from users), and (b) an adversary applying common image transformations (scaling, cropping, JPEG compression, median filtering, Gaussian smoothing, color jitter, color quantization, Gaussian noise, sharpening) to strip or evade the watermark post-generation. The paper does not model an adversary who knows the secret pattern (c, tau), does not evaluate targeted/adaptive attacks against the watermark, and explicitly flags flipping as a blind spot addressed only by an ad hoc OR-of-original-and-flipped detection heuristic.
limitations: Authors' own stated limitations: (a) computational cost from repeated sampling (to reach the match-rate threshold) plus extra backpropagation for the guidance gradient at every denoising step; (b) generation-quality gap remains, particularly for MAR models, versus the true unwatermarked reference. Additional limitations evident from the text: robustness is only tested against non-adaptive image transformations, not against an adversary aware of the watermark scheme or attempting targeted removal; flip-invariance is patched with an ad hoc OR-of-two-detections heuristic rather than a principled fix; the VAR baseline results are from the authors' own reimplementation (official inference code was unavailable) and 'differ slightly from the original paper'; robustness comparisons against Gaussian-Shading/PRC-W are restricted to EDM2 models only since those baselines are diffusion-specific, so head-to-head robustness numbers on VAR/MAR/Stable-Diffusion exist only for Luminark and the two weak post-hoc baselines, not against the strongest certified competitors.

datasets_used: MS-COCO validation set (30K samples, for Stable Diffusion 2.1 text-to-image evaluation)
quantitative_results: Detection accuracy (avg over 9 transformations): Luminark EDM2-XS 96.21%, EDM2-L 95.49%, EDM2-XXL 95.23%, VAR-d16 95.81%, VAR-d30 96.01%, VAR-d36 95.95%, MAR-Base 96.56%, MAR-Large 95.79%, MAR-Huge 95.80%
Detection accuracy baselines on EDM2-XS: DwtDct 58.47%, DwtDctSvd 65.34%, Gaussian-Shading 99.50%, PRC-W 96.79%

FID (lower better) for diffusion models, columns GS./PRC-W./Ours/Ref. in that order: EDM2-XS -- GS 6.99, PRC-W 6.88, Ours 4.40, Ref 3.53; EDM2-L -- GS 5.08, PRC-W 5.00, Ours 2.72, Ref 2.06; EDM2-XXL -- GS 4.87, PRC-W 4.60, Ours 2.13, Ref 1.81 (Gaussian-Shading and PRC-W are diffusion-specific and 'Not Applicable' to VAR/MAR)

FID for AR/hybrid models, columns Ours/Ref. only (GS/PRC-W not applicable): VAR-d16 Ours 4.04, Ref 3.47; VAR-d30 Ours 3.06, Ref 2.05; VAR-d36 Ours 4.22, Ref 2.76; MAR-Base Ours 3.88, Ref 2.31; MAR-Large Ours 3.32, Ref 1.78; MAR-Huge Ours 3.09, Ref 1.55
Stable Diffusion 2.1 / MS-COCO: FID -- Ref 26.83, Gaussian-Shading 26.27, PRC-W 26.33, Luminark 26.00; CLIP score -- Ref 0.3329, Gaussian-Shading 0.3302, PRC-W 0.3314, Luminark 0.3320

Ablation FID (EDM2-L / VAR-d30 / MAR-Large): Luminark 2.72/3.06/3.32; R-channel-only 3.01/3.08/3.50; G-only 3.39/3.26/3.49; B-only 4.30/3.74/4.89; RGB-average 3.48/3.32/3.41; random linear combo 3.03/3.52/4.06; post-hoc projection 16.70/7.68/6.88; hard step-wise projection 92.05/33.25/85.15
Average repetitions needed to reach match-rate threshold: ~2.3 for EDM2 experiment (footnote)
baselines_compared: Gaussian-Shading / GS (Yang et al. 2024, diffusion-specific certified)
